handled_foot: Your connection is your livelihood. We watch your home like it's ours.
arch_tag: the network clocks in at 9 a.m.
arch_body: Your traffic peaks in the workday, not the evening — cloud sync and video calls together made up 49% of the month. Uptime and upload stability matter more here than raw volume: a dropped second on a call costs more than a slow movie. Your network delivered, holding sub-10ms latency through the busiest hours.
macro_line: These macros say "professional, not playful." Vs. April, video conferencing rose +3 pts as cloud backups held flat — a busier month of calls.
suggestions: Have you considered a battery backup (UPS)?
Your livelihood runs over this connection. A small UPS keeps the appliance and router alive through brief outages — so a flickering power line never drops you mid-call.
Ask us → we can spec one to your gear
Have you tried locking video calls to top priority?
We can pin conferencing above everything in your traffic shaping, so a cloud backup kicking off never steals from a live Zoom.
Ask us → a 5-minute change, no cost
Have you thought about a wired link to your desk?
All-day calls are happiest off Wi-Fi. If your desk is near the appliance, a single cable removes wireless jitter entirely.
Ask us → we'll check the run
ally_intro: Your A777ance membership is also a network of people. Based on your household profile, we think you'd hit it off with a member who lives the work-from-home setup too:
ally_blurb: “I set up rock-solid home offices — UPS, wired backhaul, call-priority QoS. Happy to compare notes anytime.”
